scope.summary: Implement morph-target delta import for avatars: accept an OBJ morph-delta file (vertex displacements) against a registered base AVT- mesh, compute the displaced body proxy geometry, and persist the result as a secondary BPX- row linked to the same AVT-
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/tailor/avatars/{avt_id}/morph accepts an OBJ delta file and a morph_key string
The displaced mesh vertices are applied to the base mesh, extract_measurements() is re-run, and a new BPX- row is written with mode='capsule'
The AVT- row's blend_shapes JSONB is updated with the new (morph_key, 1.0, bpx_id) entry
An invalid delta (mismatched vertex count) returns HTTP 422 with a descriptive error
scope.risk_if_missed: Operators using Daz 3D, Character Creator, or similar tools that export body deltas as OBJ morphs cannot load shape variants without re-importing the full mesh each time. This forces redundant full-mesh uploads for each body-weight or expression variant.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
